blocks.content: Hostile Friendly Relations (6)

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Fine. If you're that curious, I'll introduce it specially to you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I'm not really….
Before Shirone could finish speaking, Zion extended his hand towards the sword.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Come, Armand.
A clear ringing of metal spread throughout the room.
The moment Shirone turned towards Armand, the sword floated up on its own and was drawn into Zion's hand.
Shirone, who had twisted his body again along the path of the sword, suddenly stopped moving.
Before he knew it, the blade was at his throat.
The cold metal hummed, sending high-pitched vibrations to his ears.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Irritated by the sudden interruption of her hobby, Uorin grumbled.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Oppa! Why are you acting like this all of a sudden?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: You stay still.
This time, even Zion wasn't willing to back down.
Glaring at Shirone, his lips curled in annoyance.
The fact that he wasn't showing any fear, even with a blade at his neck, was driving him mad.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What's wrong? Suddenly lost for words? Afraid I'll kill you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone's nerves were on edge, feeling the vibration from beneath his chin.
He had already unfolded his Spirit Zone.
The nature of Armand, conveyed through synesthesia, was truly bizarre.
Was this really a sword, or a living organism?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Why aren't you answering? Weren't you supposed to be some genius mage?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was a petty remark to make after ambushing someone.
Of course, Shirone was confident he could cast Teleportation faster than Zion's reaction speed.
But he couldn't confidently say he would be able to evade the sword flying through the air afterward.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Hahaha! Of course, you couldn't. My guess was right. Articles in magazines are all exaggerated. You're just a student; what greatest talent could you possibly have...

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Oppa, that's too much.
Zion paid no attention and threw Armand.
The demon sword emitting a chilling light swiftly and covertly flew back to its original place and stood upright.
Shirone belatedly realized that there had been no stand supporting the sword from the beginning.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Do you know why I don't kill you? Because you're not even worth it. Even if it's not me, there are plenty of people in the royal palace who hate you. You probably felt it, right? This morning.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone couldn't make sense of Zion's words.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you talking about? What about this morning?
This time, it was Zion who tilted his head.
Although he couldn't stand Shirone, he knew better than anyone that Shirone was not stupid.
In the end, it meant that Reina, the legal representative, had preemptively blocked information from reaching Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Hmm, she's not ordinary either.
He underestimated her because she wasn't the head of the household, but she turned out to be a more resourceful woman than he expected.
Well, that must be why she was sent by Ogent Family.
In any case, it was clear that she didn't want last night's incident to come to light.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Hmph, now I see you're really a pitiful bastard. In the end, even your allies don't trust you. You're probably the only one in the entire palace who doesn't know about what happened last night.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So I ask you, what happened!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: From what Zion was saying, it seemed like Reina knew about it.
How could she know something that he didn't?
It wasn't likely that he was deliberately kept in the dark.
But what could have prevented even Amy from sending a message?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Forget it. I'll go ask her myself.
Shirone, realizing he wouldn't get answers from Zion, headed towards the door.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Even if you go, you probably won't be able to meet her. I heard she moved quarters last night.
Shirone paused with his hand on the doorknob and turned to look at Uorin.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Moved? Where to?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: I haven't heard that either. Your legal representative added a non-disclosure clause early in the morning. You'll probably find out if you go to the audience chamber.
She suggested he ask the highest authority directly.
Moreover, a non-disclosure clause. It wasn't a powerful diplomatic means, but from Reina's position as the relatively weaker party, it was a bold move.
Finally sensing that the situation was seriously wrong, Shirone left the gallery without looking back.
An awkward silence flowed in the gallery after Shirone left.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: You, whose side are you on?

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: What are you talking about all of a sudden? Side?
Uorin responded with an innocent expression.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Why are you being so nice to Shirone? That bastard is my enemy! An enemy who's trying to steal the throne! You should obviously be helping me!
Zion eventually exploded with anger.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Why are you acting like this, Oppa? We've never had a conversation like this before. Don't tell me you were nice to me expecting that? Just because I'm Terraje's daughter?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: No, that's not it….
Zion's face flushed with embarrassment.
No one would understand how humiliating it was to hear something like this from his own sister.
In fact, her mere presence was helpful.
Just by flaunting the title of being Uorin's older brother, any noble would eagerly spare no effort to support him.
With that overwhelming power, Zion had eliminated countless formidable enemies over the years.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Tch! So this is how you're going to play it?
Zion turned around with a bitter heart and blew out his breath heavily.
Sensing that he was acting as if he would never speak to her again, Wuorin sighed and gently patted her sulking brother's back.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Ei, come on. I get it. Do you think I haven't thought about it? It's just awkward for us to have this kind of conversation. The number one reason for royalty to split apart is this. There's no one here who can threaten you anyway. What are you so afraid of?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Zion found himself wondering the same thing.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: What exactly was he afraid of?

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Alright. I was being petty. I'm sorry.
Zion immediately changed his attitude.
He felt better knowing that his sister had understood his concerns.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Uorin said a few more things that Geon had always wanted to hear and then returned to her seat.
Unlike Zion, whose mouth was stretched from ear to ear, she poured tea as if she was tired, then sighed a long breath towards the ceiling.

blocks.speaker: Uorin
blocks.content: Phew, men are really.
2. The Trap of Choosing Between Two Choices (1)
Shirone went to the administrative department to request an audience.
Permission was granted within 10 minutes. It was such a quick process that it felt like there had been instructions.
Upon arriving at the audience room, the royal guard opened the door.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: I was just about to summon you. Come this way.
Shirone sat across from Orkamph.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Your family's quarters have been moved to the annex. I'll have someone guide you there.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The swift explanation almost sounded like a defense.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What exactly is it that I don't know?
Orkamph let out a sigh.
Being king meant there was nowhere to hide.
Reina might have been able to escape difficult questions, but he couldn't.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: From last evening until dawn, there were five assassination attempts. Three poisonings and two by assassins. Lethal doses of poison were found in the food meant for your family. In the early hours, assassins were caught entering the royal quarter at one-hour intervals. We couldn't investigate the mastermind because they committed suicide, but it is a well-known fact that they were after you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone felt as though he had been struck on the head with a hammer.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why would someone try to kill us?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: What do you mean? Did you really think nothing would happen?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: To be honest... he did.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I don't understand. Assassinating a candidate for the first prince is a serious crime. If the mastermind is revealed, it would be more than enough to destroy their family. Does it make sense to resolve such a grave matter in just 2 days?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Do you think they're foolish? No, Shirone. On the contrary, this is a very logical and clever move. They're exploiting a blind spot in your thinking.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Blind spot... in thinking?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Assassins are divided into two types: assassins and hitmen. There's a saying among traditional assassins: 'It's not the person that kills, but the situation.' You can dodge a blade, but you can't escape a situation. If they had to kill you, last night was the perfect opportunity.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone corrected his error in his line of thought.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: An assassin's skill doesn't lie in wielding a blade but in creating the situation. That's why, in a royal palace, a single assassin is far more terrifying than a hundred guards. They pierce the blind spot in your thinking. Once you're caught, you have no choice but to offer your neck even while knowing.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Shirone, stop trying to analyze everything with your head. This palace is filled with predators. No matter how great the price of failure is, no coward here would miss their chance to act out of fear.
Orkamph rose from his seat and walked over to his desk.
As he poured strong liquor into a crystal glass, he said,

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Reina made the right judgment. Anyway, in 3 days you can become the official successor. There's no point in engaging in a mud fight, it'll only be a loss for us. So you too, pretend you don't know.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone clenched his fist.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Are you telling me to just take it? We could use this as an opportunity to strike back.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Then let me ask you. Who do you think is behind this?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Of course, Terraje Faction….

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Was it really only Terraje Faction targeting him? That was also a blind spot in his thinking that he had not considered until now.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: ...I don't know.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: That's right, you don't know. It is no exaggeration to say that changing the first prince candidate is a matter that moves all the interests of the kingdom. Even those outside Terraje Faction would have plenty of reasons to eliminate you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone tried to narrow down the suspects.
The faces of numerous people flashed by.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even Orkamph….

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Does everyone want me to die?

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: If it's Terraje Faction behind this, it's likely a warning. If it's Neutral Faction, then they're testing the waters. They want an accurate source to know what kind of person you are.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A warning? It was a lethal dose of poison. If we had ingested it, we would have died. Even Amy, who came along without any fault, almost died.
Orkamph gulped down the strong liquor.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone was overly agitated, but perhaps that was normal.
For the average person, assassination was a concept too distant to grasp.
One might be robbed or hit by a carriage, but living each day knowing that a professional killer is targeting you? It would drive anyone insane in less than a month.
Such was the life Orkamph had lived.
He lived his whole life cohabiting with assassins, eating, going to the bathroom, and even having children.
Being able to forget it all with a sip of strong liquor—he realized, perhaps he was the abnormal one.
